verses.latin_vulgate: Verbum quod factum est ad Jeremiam a Domino, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַדָּבָר֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָיָ֣ה אֶֽל־יִרְמְיָ֔הוּ מֵאֵ֥ת יְהֹוָ֖ה לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: The word which came to Jeremiah from the Lord, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Surge, et descende in domum figuli, et ibi audies verba mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֥וּם וְיָרַדְתָּ֖ בֵּ֣ית הַיּוֹצֵ֑ר וְשָׁ֖מָּה אַשְׁמִיעֲךָ֥ אֶת־דְּבָרָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Arise, and go down to the potter’s house, and there I will cause thee to hear my words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendi in domum figuli, et ecce ipse faciebat opus super rotam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֵרֵ֖ד בֵּ֣ית הַיּוֹצֵ֑ר (והנהו) [וְהִנֵּה־ה֛וּא] עֹשֶׂ֥ה מְלָאכָ֖ה עַל־הָאׇבְנָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: Then I went down to the potter’s house, and, behold, he wrought a work on the wheels.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dissipatum est vas quod ipse faciebat e luto manibus suis : conversusque fecit illud vas alterum, sicut placuerat in oculis ejus ut faceret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִשְׁחַ֣ת הַכְּלִ֗י אֲשֶׁ֨ר ה֥וּא עֹשֶׂ֛ה בַּחֹ֖מֶר בְּיַ֣ד הַיּוֹצֵ֑ר וְשָׁ֗ב וַֽיַּעֲשֵׂ֙הוּ֙ כְּלִ֣י אַחֵ֔ר כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר יָשַׁ֛ר בְּעֵינֵ֥י הַיּוֹצֵ֖ר לַעֲשֽׂוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And the vessel that he made of clay was marred in the hand of the potter: so he made it again another vessel, as seemed good to the potter to make it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלַ֥י לֵאמֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid sicut figulus iste,  non potero vobis facere, domus Israël ? ait Dominus :  ecce sicut lutum in manu figuli,  sic vos in manu mea, domus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲכַיּוֹצֵ֨ר הַזֶּ֜ה לֹא־אוּכַ֨ל לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת לָכֶ֛ם בֵּ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה הִנֵּ֤ה כַחֹ֙מֶר֙ בְּיַ֣ד הַיּוֹצֵ֔ר כֵּן־אַתֶּ֥ם בְּיָדִ֖י בֵּ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: O house of Israel, cannot I do with you as this potter? saith the Lord. Behold, as the clay is in the potter’s hand, so are ye in mine hand, O house of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Repente loquar adversum gentem et adversus regnum,  ut eradicem, et destruam, et disperdam illud :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֶ֣גַע אֲדַבֵּ֔ר עַל־גּ֖וֹי וְעַל־מַמְלָכָ֑ה לִנְת֥וֹשׁ וְלִנְת֖וֹץ וּֽלְהַאֲבִֽיד׃
verses.king_james: At what instant I shall speak concerning a nation, and concerning a kingdom, to pluck up, and to pull down, and to destroy it;

verses.latin_vulgate: si pœnitentiam egerit gens illa a malo suo  quod locutus sum adversus eam,  agam et ego pœnitentiam super malo  quod cogitavi ut facerem ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָׁב֙ הַגּ֣וֹי הַה֔וּא מֵרָ֣עָת֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבַּ֖רְתִּי עָלָ֑יו וְנִֽחַמְתִּי֙ עַל־הָ֣רָעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר חָשַׁ֖בְתִּי לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת לֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: If that nation, against whom I have pronounced, turn from their evil, I will repent of the evil that I thought to do unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et subito loquar de gente et de regno,  ut ædificem et plantem illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרֶ֣גַע אֲדַבֵּ֔ר עַל־גּ֖וֹי וְעַל־מַמְלָכָ֑ה לִבְנ֖וֹת וְלִנְטֽוֹעַ׃
verses.king_james: And at what instant I shall speak concerning a nation, and concerning a kingdom, to build and to plant it;

verses.latin_vulgate: Si fecerit malum in oculis meis,  ut non audiat vocem meam,  pœnitentiam agam super bono quod locutus sum ut facerem ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָשָׂ֤ה (הרעה) [הָרַע֙] בְּעֵינַ֔י לְבִלְתִּ֖י שְׁמֹ֣עַ בְּקוֹלִ֑י וְנִֽחַמְתִּי֙ עַל־הַטּוֹבָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָמַ֖רְתִּי לְהֵיטִ֥יב אוֹתֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: If it do evil in my sight, that it obey not my voice, then I will repent of the good, wherewith I said I would benefit them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo dic viro Juda, et habitatoribus Jerusalem, dicens :  Hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego fingo contra vos malum,  et cogito contra vos cogitationem :  revertatur unusquisque a via sua mala,  et dirigite vias vestras et studia vestra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֡ה אֱמׇר־נָ֣א אֶל־אִישׁ־יְהוּדָה֩ וְעַל־יוֹשְׁבֵ֨י יְרוּשָׁלַ֜͏ִם לֵאמֹ֗ר כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנֵּ֨ה אָנֹכִ֜י יוֹצֵ֤ר עֲלֵיכֶם֙ רָעָ֔ה וְחֹשֵׁ֥ב עֲלֵיכֶ֖ם מַחֲשָׁבָ֑ה שׁ֣וּבוּ נָ֗א אִ֚ישׁ מִדַּרְכּ֣וֹ הָרָעָ֔ה וְהֵיטִ֥יבוּ דַרְכֵיכֶ֖ם וּמַעַלְלֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Now therefore go to, speak to the men of Judah, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith the Lord; Behold, I frame evil against you, and devise a device against you: return ye now every one from his evil way, and make your ways and your doings good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixerunt : Desperavimus :  post cogitationes enim nostras ibimus,  et unusquisque pravitatem cordis sui mali faciemus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמְר֖וּ נוֹאָ֑שׁ כִּֽי־אַחֲרֵ֤י מַחְשְׁבוֹתֵ֙ינוּ֙ נֵלֵ֔ךְ וְאִ֛ישׁ שְׁרִר֥וּת לִבּֽוֹ־הָרָ֖ע נַעֲשֶֽׂה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And they said, There is no hope: but we will walk after our own devices, and we will every one do the imagination of his evil heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo hæc dicit Dominus :  Interrogate gentes :  Quis audivit talia horribilia,  quæ fecit nimis virgo Israël ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה שַֽׁאֲלוּ־נָא֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם מִ֥י שָׁמַ֖ע כָּאֵ֑לֶּה שַֽׁעֲרֻרִת֙ עָשְׂתָ֣ה מְאֹ֔ד בְּתוּלַ֖ת יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Therefore thus saith the Lord; Ask ye now among the heathen, who hath heard such things: the virgin of Israel hath done a very horrible thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid deficiet de petra agri nix Libani ?  aut evelli possunt aquæ erumpentes frigidæ, et defluentes ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיַעֲזֹ֥ב מִצּ֛וּר שָׂדַ֖י שֶׁ֣לֶג לְבָנ֑וֹן אִם־יִנָּֽתְשׁ֗וּ מַ֛יִם זָרִ֥ים קָרִ֖ים נוֹזְלִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Will a man leave the snow of Lebanon which cometh from the rock of the field? or shall the cold flowing waters that come from another place be forsaken?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia oblitus est mei populus meus,  frustra libantes,  et impingentes in viis suis,  in semitis sæculi,  ut ambularent per eas in itinere non trito,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־שְׁכֵחֻ֥נִי עַמִּ֖י לַשָּׁ֣וְא יְקַטֵּ֑רוּ וַיַּכְשִׁל֤וּם בְּדַרְכֵיהֶם֙ שְׁבִילֵ֣י עוֹלָ֔ם לָלֶ֣כֶת נְתִיב֔וֹת דֶּ֖רֶךְ לֹ֥א סְלוּלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Because my people hath forgotten me, they have burned incense to vanity, and they have caused them to stumble in their ways from the ancient paths, to walk in paths, in a way not cast up;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut fieret terra eorum in desolationem,  et in sibilum sempiternum :  omnis qui præterierit per eam obstupescet,  et movebit caput suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָשׂ֥וּם אַרְצָ֛ם לְשַׁמָּ֖ה (שרוקת) [שְׁרִיק֣וֹת] עוֹלָ֑ם כֹּ֚ל עוֹבֵ֣ר עָלֶ֔יהָ יִשֹּׁ֖ם וְיָנִ֥יד בְּרֹאשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: To make their land desolate, and a perpetual hissing; every one that passeth thereby shall be astonished, and wag his head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut ventus urens dispergam eos coram inimico :  dorsum, et non faciem, ostendam eis in die perditionis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּרוּחַ־קָדִ֥ים אֲפִיצֵ֖ם לִפְנֵ֣י אוֹיֵ֑ב עֹ֧רֶף וְלֹֽא־פָנִ֛ים אֶרְאֵ֖ם בְּי֥וֹם אֵידָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I will scatter them as with an east wind before the enemy; I will shew them the back, and not the face, in the day of their calamity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt :   Venite, et cogitemus contra Jeremiam cogitationes :  non enim peribit lex a sacerdote,  neque consilium a sapiente,  nec sermo a propheta :  venite, et percutiamus eum lingua,  et non attendamus ad universos sermones ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֗וּ לְכ֨וּ וְנַחְשְׁבָ֥ה עַֽל־יִרְמְיָ֘הוּ֮ מַחֲשָׁבוֹת֒ כִּי֩ לֹא־תֹאבַ֨ד תּוֹרָ֜ה מִכֹּהֵ֗ן וְעֵצָה֙ מֵחָכָ֔ם וְדָבָ֖ר מִנָּבִ֑יא לְכוּ֙ וְנַכֵּ֣הוּ בַלָּשׁ֔וֹן וְאַל־נַקְשִׁ֖יבָה אֶל־כׇּל־דְּבָרָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Then said they, Come, and let us devise devices against Jeremiah; for the law shall not perish from the priest, nor counsel from the wise, nor the word from the prophet. Come, and let us smite him with the tongue, and let us not give heed to any of his words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attende, Domine, ad me,  et audi vocem adversariorum meorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַקְשִׁ֥יבָה יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלָ֑י וּשְׁמַ֖ע לְק֥וֹל יְרִיבָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Give heed to me, O Lord, and hearken to the voice of them that contend with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid redditur pro bono malum,  quia foderunt foveam animæ meæ ?  Recordare quod steterim in conspectu tuo  ut loquerer pro eis bonum,  et averterem indignationem tuam ab eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַיְשֻׁלַּ֤ם תַּֽחַת־טוֹבָה֙ רָעָ֔ה כִּי־כָר֥וּ שׁוּחָ֖ה לְנַפְשִׁ֑י זְכֹ֣ר ׀ עׇמְדִ֣י לְפָנֶ֗יךָ לְדַבֵּ֤ר עֲלֵיהֶם֙ טוֹבָ֔ה לְהָשִׁ֥יב אֶת־חֲמָתְךָ֖ מֵהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Shall evil be recompensed for good? for they have digged a pit for my soul. Remember that I stood before thee to speak good for them, and to turn away thy wrath from them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea da filios eorum in famem,  et deduc eos in manus gladii :  fiant uxores eorum absque liberis, et viduæ :  et viri earum interficiantur morte :  juvenes eorum confodiantur gladio in prælio :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵן֩ תֵּ֨ן אֶת־בְּנֵיהֶ֜ם לָרָעָ֗ב וְהַגִּרֵם֮ עַל־יְדֵי־חֶ֒רֶב֒ וְתִהְיֶ֨נָה נְשֵׁיהֶ֤ם שַׁכֻּלוֹת֙ וְאַלְמָנ֔וֹת וְאַ֨נְשֵׁיהֶ֔ם יִהְי֖וּ הֲרֻ֣גֵי מָ֑וֶת בַּח֣וּרֵיהֶ֔ם מֻכֵּי־חֶ֖רֶב בַּמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Therefore deliver up their children to the famine, and pour out their blood by the force of the sword; and let their wives be bereaved of their children, and be widows; and let their men be put to death; let their young men be slain by the sword in battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: audiatur clamor de domibus eorum :  adduces enim super eos latronem repente,  quia foderunt foveam ut caperent me,  et laqueos absconderunt pedibus meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּשָּׁמַ֤ע זְעָקָה֙ מִבָּ֣תֵּיהֶ֔ם כִּֽי־תָבִ֧יא עֲלֵיהֶ֛ם גְּד֖וּד פִּתְאֹ֑ם כִּֽי־כָר֤וּ (שיחה) [שׁוּחָה֙] לְלׇכְדֵ֔נִי וּפַחִ֖ים טָמְנ֥וּ לְרַגְלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Let a cry be heard from their houses, when thou shalt bring a troop suddenly upon them: for they have digged a pit to take me, and hid snares for my feet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu autem, Domine, scis omne consilium eorum  adversum me in mortem :  ne propitieris iniquitati eorum,  et peccatum eorum a facie tua non deleatur :  fiant corruentes in conspectu tuo ;  in tempore furoris tui abutere eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתָּ֣ה יְ֠הֹוָ֠ה יָדַ֜עְתָּ אֶֽת־כׇּל־עֲצָתָ֤ם עָלַי֙ לַמָּ֔וֶת אַל־תְּכַפֵּר֙ עַל־עֲוֺנָ֔ם וְחַטָּאתָ֖ם מִלְּפָנֶ֣יךָ אַל־תֶּ֑מְחִי (והיו) [וְיִהְי֤וּ] מֻכְשָׁלִים֙ לְפָנֶ֔יךָ בְּעֵ֥ת אַפְּךָ֖ עֲשֵׂ֥ה בָהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Yet, Lord, thou knowest all their counsel against me to slay me: forgive not their iniquity, neither blot out their sin from thy sight, but let them be overthrown before thee; deal thus with them in the time of thine anger.
